title: Caramelized Onion, Apple & Sage Sausage Breakfast Bake
description: A crustless breakfast casserole with deeply caramelized onions, sausage, diced apple, and melted cheese over a sage egg custard. Great for make-ahead brunches.

ingredients.name: breakfast sausage, pork or turkey (apple-sage variety works especially well)
medium apple, small dice (Honeycrisp for sweeter, Granny Smith for more tart)
sharp white cheddar or smoked gouda, shredded
fresh sage, finely chopped (or 3/4 tsp dried)
nutmeg or splash of Dijon mustard (optional)

instructions.text: Caramelize the onions: heat olive oil or butter in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add onions with a pinch of salt and cook 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deeply golden and sweet. Don't rush this step -- it's the flavor backbone.
In a separate skillet, brown the sausage until cooked through. Add diced apple during the last 2–3 minutes so it softens slightly but keeps its shape. Remove from heat.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease an 8x8 or 9x13 baking dish.
Spread sausage and apple mixture evenly in the dish. Top with caramelized onions. Sprinkle cheese evenly over everything.
Whisk together eggs, milk, sage, salt, pepper, and nutmeg or Dijon if using. Pour evenly over the casserole.
Bake uncovered 35–40 minutes (an 8x8 dish may take 40–45 minutes) until the center is set, the top is lightly golden, and a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
Rest 5–10 minutes before slicing. Serve warm.

notes:
Make-ahead: assemble fully, cover and refrigerate overnight, bake in the morning. Add 5 minutes to bake time if going straight from the fridge.

Cheese swaps: Gruyère adds more savory depth; smoked gouda adds a subtle smokiness that works well with apple.

For a lighter version: use turkey sausage and whole milk instead of half-and-half.

Extra cozy variation: add a pinch of cinnamon or allspice to the egg mixture -- use a very light hand.
